translation: The court held that, according to Article 25, Paragraph 1, Item 3 of the Provisions of the Supreme People's Court on Several Issues concerning the Handling of Execution Objections and Reconsideration Cases by the People's Courts, "where a person other than the party concerned raises an execution objection to a bank deposit that is subject to compulsory enforcement by the people's court, the people's court shall determine whether the objector is the right holder based on the account name recorded by the financial institution." In this case, the person subject to enforcement, Shi Mou, had an execution case that was not fully performed, and the court's freezing of the deposit account of the person subject to enforcement, Shi Mou, was in compliance with legal provisions. The 500,000 yuan payment for goods that the persons other than the parties concerned, Zhan Mou and Chen Mou, claimed to have been remitted into the deposit account of the person subject to enforcement, Shi Mou, was made with their prior knowledge that the account into which they remitted was a bank account in the name of the person subject to enforcement; this act does not constitute a mistaken remittance or erroneous remittance. Their claim that the money belonged to them lacks legal basis and is not supported by the court. Accordingly, the court rejected the objection requests of the persons other than the parties concerned, Zhan Mou and Chen Mou.
